summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Anxiety and insomnia, with a history of significant anxiety episodes.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing anxiety and insomnia, particularly during periods of stress, such as during the initial phases of the COVID-19 pandemic when she was working as a healthcare provider. She notes that her anxiety was particularly severe in November and December of 2020, coinciding with the onset of her progesterone therapy. She reports that the anxiety often started during the day at work and was exacerbated by the demands of her job. She mentions that she felt selfish and guilty because she wasn't on the front lines, despite experiencing anxiety. She has been taking buspar since Dr. Brown prescribed it and reports that it has been helpful. She stopped taking the camila birth control recently, and has noticed increased hunger and a feeling of being unsettled. She has experienced breast tenderness, particularly in the left breast, and is concerned about potential breast cancer risk. She reports that her mom was diagnosed with breast cancer around 2009. She has been vaccinated and her husband and son have been vaccinated as well. She is worried about her risk of breast cancer given her family history and her own symptoms.

Findings
Examination reveals some palpable breast lumps in the left breast, which are slightly smaller than previously reported but still present. The patient's facial skin appears somewhat thinner than previously. The patient's respiratory rate is 16, and her heart rate is 88 beats per minute. The patient reports no other significant findings on physical exam.

Assessment
The patient presents with anxiety and insomnia, with a history of significant anxiety episodes. She also has palpable breast lumps in the left breast, raising concern for potential malignancy. Her family history of breast cancer further increases her risk.

Plan
I will order a breast specialist referral to evaluate the breast lumps and perform a follow-up mammogram. I will also refill the patient's camila birth control prescription. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess the patient's progress and discuss any further concerns.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Follow-up bilateral breast cysts.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Donna Torres is a 40-year-old female who presents for follow-up surveillance of bilateral breast cysts.

The patient reports continual presence of “lumps” in bilateral breasts with some soreness. She is being seen today for surveillance of the cysts. The patient is taking progesterone, which was initially prescribed to support normalization of menstruation, which was effective. She is also still using vaginal cream one time per week which has improved symptoms of itching and discomfort.

Her last pap smear was in 2019, with negative/negative results. She did have an abnormal pap smear in 2009, which resulted in repeat pap smears per 6 months until her results normalized.

Mrs. Torres experienced increased anxiety in 11/2021 and 12/2021, stating “it was brutal” and felt she could not manage her symptoms and sought treatment. Dr. Brown started the patient on Buspar, which has successfully controlled her anxiety. The patient relates her anxiety to work related stress and possibly aging. Her work involves supporting Covid evaluation centers and clinics. She confirms receiving both Covid vaccinations. Initially, she was experiencing spikes in anxiety prior to menstruation, but her anxiety then became more random with an unclear cause. To note, the patient was started on Singulair, which the patient was told could also cause anxiety.

The patient discontinued Camila birth control, which did result in a significant increase in appetite. This has resolved and she reports she has lost weight in the past 6 months.

Gynecologic History
Last pap smear: 2019.
History of abnormal pap smear: 2009.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
Bilateral breast cysts.
Anxiety.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Employed in healthcare, supports Covid evaluation centers and clinics.

FAMILY HISTORY

Mother, deceased age 45, from complications of breast cancer.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Buspar.
Singulair.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Genitourinary
Breast: Breast density.

ASSESSMENT

• Follow-up bilateral breast cysts.
• Pap smear.

PLAN

Follow-up bilateral breast cysts
Physical exam indicated stable breast density bilaterally. The patient is being seen regularly for monitoring. The plan is to continue close monitoring and the patient will be referred to breast specialist for additional review. The patient agreed to this plan.

Pap smear
Exam was normal. No additional treatment needed at this time.

INSTRUCTIONS

Schedule appointment with breast specialist. Call clinic with any additional concerns.

section_miscategorizations.reason: This is phrased as the patient's report rather than an examiner's observation. However, it functions as a summary of negative exam findings, so the impact of the misplacement is limited.
